claims.evidence_text: F . culmorum is a soil-, air- and seed-borne fungal pathogen of small-grain cereals causing foot and root rot, fusarium seedling blight, and especially FHB, a disease leading to decreased yield and mycotoxin contamination of grain
claims.claim_summary: Fusarium culmorum is a soil-, air- and seed-borne pathogen of wheat causing foot/root rot, seedling blight and Fusarium head blight (FHB) with yield loss and mycotoxin contamination

claims.evidence_text: Fungicide treatments at anthesis can reduce disease levels by only 15–30% [ 24 ]
claims.claim_summary: Fungicide treatments at anthesis reduce Fusarium head blight disease levels in wheat by only 15-30%

claims.evidence_text: Fungicide treatments at anthesis can reduce disease levels by only 15–30% [ 24 ]
claims.claim_summary: Anthesis fungicide application achieves only 15-30% reduction of Fusarium head blight in wheat

claims.evidence_text: Because no fully resistant cultivars exist, control of diseases caused by Fusarium spp. must be achieved by such agricultural management practices as crop rotation and postharvest debris removal to diminish inoculum pressure [ 23 ]
claims.claim_summary: No fully resistant wheat cultivars exist against Fusarium spp. (FHB); control relies on management not host resistance

claims.evidence_text: control of diseases caused by Fusarium spp. must be achieved by such agricultural management practices as crop rotation and postharvest debris removal to diminish inoculum pressure [ 23 ]
claims.claim_summary: Crop rotation and postharvest debris removal are recommended to diminish Fusarium spp. inoculum pressure in wheat

claims.evidence_text: wheat inoculated with M . bolleyi showed significantly weaker symptoms on leaves from infection by fungus F . culmorum than did plants without the endophyte
claims.claim_summary: Root inoculation of wheat with endophyte Microdochium bolleyi reduced Fusarium culmorum leaf symptom severity (biological control potential)
